title: heracleum sphondylium var. aestivum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. aestivum, also known as wild celery or cow parsley, is a biennial plant in the carrot family that grows in Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Heracleum sphondylium var. aestivum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
